title: Schiller CARDIOVIT AT Series Electrocardiograph (EKG) Machine - Battery Not Charging
description: EKG not charging, running only on battery, or shutting down due to power source, battery, or connection issues.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: If the device is in use, transfer the patient to another EKG machine before troubleshooting.

steps.title: Verify Wall Outlet Power
steps.instructions: Plug in another device to confirm power. Try a different outlet if needed.

steps.title: Inspect AC Power Cord and Adapter
steps.instructions: Ensure connections are fully seated at both ends. Look for frayed wires, bent prongs, or loose connectors. Swap with a known good cord/adapter if available.

steps.title: Confirm AC Power Indicator on Device
steps.instructions: Plug in the device and observe indicator lights. Power the unit on if possible.

steps.title: Check Battery Charging Status on Display
steps.instructions: Look for charging symbol or percentage increase. Note any battery-related warnings or messages.

steps.title: Perform a Power Reset
steps.instructions: Power off the unit. Disconnect AC power. Wait 30–60 seconds. Reconnect power and restart.

steps.title: Check for Docking Station or External Charger (If Applicable)
steps.instructions: Ensure the device is properly seated. Inspect docking contacts for dirt or damage. Try a different dock if available.

steps.title: Inspect for Environmental or Physical Issues
steps.instructions: Check for excessive heat near battery area. Look for liquid exposure or corrosion.

CCR.Complaint: EKG machine not charging and shutting down during use.
CCR.Cause: Battery failed to accept charge after verifying power cord, outlet, and adapter were functional.
CCR.Resolution: Removed device from service and sent to Clinical Engineering for battery replacement and evaluation.

title: Schiller CARDIOVIT AT Series Electrocardiograph (EKG) Machine - Touchscreen or Keypad Not Responding
description: Troubleshooting unresponsive touchscreen or keypad issues on Schiller CARDIOVIT AT Series EKG machines.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: If the device is in use, stop acquisition and move the patient to another functioning EKG machine.

steps.title: Verify Device Power and Screen Activity
steps.instructions: Confirm power LED indicators are on, look for screen backlight or faint image, and attempt to power the unit off and back on.

steps.title: Perform a Full Reboot
steps.instructions: Shut down the device completely, disconnect AC power for 30 seconds, then reconnect and power back on.

steps.title: Inspect Touchscreen Surface or Keypad Condition
steps.instructions: Ensure screen is clean and dry, remove gloves or moisture, and inspect keypad for stuck or damaged buttons.

steps.title: Check for Screen Lock or Software Mode Restrictions
steps.instructions: Look for lock icons, attempt alternate controls, and verify the interface is not frozen.

steps.title: Test External Input (if applicable)
steps.instructions: Connect a compatible external keyboard or input device if supported.

steps.title: Inspect for Signs of Physical Damage or Liquid Intrusion
steps.instructions: Check for cracks, residue, fluid exposure, or unusual behavior like ghost touches.

CCR.Complaint: EKG touchscreen not responding to input during patient setup.
CCR.Cause: Touchscreen unresponsive after reboot; no external damage noted.
CCR.Resolution: Removed from service and sent for evaluation of touchscreen interface.

helpfulDetails: Signs of physical damage or contamination

title: Schiller CARDIOVIT AT Series Electrocardiograph (EKG) Machine - Lead Fail or Poor Signal Quality
description: Troubleshooting lead fail messages and poor ECG signal quality caused by electrodes, cables, or connections.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: If the ECG is being used for diagnosis, ensure the patient is stable and move to another functioning EKG machine if needed.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Issue
steps.instructions: Attempt to acquire an ECG and observe waveform, checking for lead fail alerts, no signal vs noisy signal, and baseline wander or artifact.

steps.title: Inspect Electrode Placement and Condition
steps.instructions: Confirm proper placement, replace dried or loose electrodes, and clean the skin to ensure good contact.

steps.title: Check Lead Wire Connections (Patient Cable)
steps.instructions: Ensure all leads are firmly connected, inspect for damage or corrosion, and reattach any loose connections.

steps.title: Inspect and Swap Patient Cable (Lead Set)
steps.instructions: Check for cable damage, manipulate cable while observing waveform, and swap with a known good cable if available.

steps.title: Check Trunk Cable Connection to the Unit
steps.instructions: Ensure the connector is fully seated, inspect for bent pins or debris, and reseat the cable.

steps.title: Minimize Electrical Interference
steps.instructions: Move cables away from power sources, prevent patient contact with metal surfaces, and reduce nearby electrical devices.

steps.title: Check Filter and Gain Settings
steps.instructions: Verify correct filter settings and adjust gain to ensure a clear and readable waveform.

steps.title: Perform System Reset (If Needed)
steps.instructions: Power cycle the unit, reconnect cables, and attempt ECG acquisition again.

CCR.Complaint: EKG showing lead fail and poor waveform quality during acquisition.
CCR.Cause: Worn patient cable with intermittent internal break causing signal loss.
CCR.Resolution: Replaced patient lead cable and verified proper signal acquisition on all leads.

helpfulDetails: Final device status (returned to service or removed)

title: Schiller CARDIOVIT AT Series Electrocardiograph (EKG) Machine - Thermal Printer Failure or Paper Feed Errors
description: Troubleshooting printer not feeding paper, paper jams, blank prints, or poor print quality on Schiller CARDIOVIT AT Series EKG machines.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: If the device is in use, stop ECG acquisition and move the patient to another EKG machine if printing is required. Expected: Patient care continues without interruption. Why it matters: Printing issues should not delay diagnosis or documentation.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Issue
steps.instructions: Attempt to print a test ECG or stored report. Observe: No paper feed, paper stops mid-print, blank or faded output, error messages on display. Expected: Issue is reproducible and clearly identified. Why it matters: Confirms whether this is a feed issue, jam, or print quality problem.

steps.title: Check Paper Supply and Type
steps.instructions: Open the printer compartment and inspect the paper roll. Ensure paper is present and not empty. Confirm correct thermal paper type for Schiller AT Series. Check that paper width matches device requirements. Expected: Proper paper is installed. Why it matters: Incorrect or missing paper is a common cause of feed and print issues.

steps.title: Verify Paper Orientation
steps.instructions: Confirm the thermal paper is loaded correctly. Ensure paper feeds from the correct direction (thermal side aligned with print head). Reload paper if unsure. Expected: Paper feeds smoothly when printing. Why it matters: Incorrect orientation results in blank prints or no output.

steps.title: Inspect for Paper Jam or Obstruction
steps.instructions: Check inside the printer compartment. Remove any jammed or torn paper. Ensure no debris is blocking the feed path. Gently clear obstructions without forcing components. Expected: Paper path is clear and unobstructed. Why it matters: Even small obstructions can prevent proper feeding.

steps.title: Check Printer Cover and Latch
steps.instructions: Ensure the printer door is fully closed and latched. Open and firmly close the printer cover. Listen/feel for proper latch engagement. Expected: Printer recognizes the cover as closed and allows printing. Why it matters: Many units will not print if the cover is not properly secured.

steps.title: Perform a Test Feed or Calibration
steps.instructions: Use the device’s feed or test print function. Press paper feed button (if available). Run internal test print if accessible. Expected: Paper advances smoothly and prints clearly. Why it matters: Helps isolate feed mechanism vs system issue.

steps.title: Inspect Print Quality (If Feeding Occurs)
steps.instructions: If paper feeds but print is poor: Look for faded, uneven, or missing print. Clean print head using approved method (lint-free cloth if accessible). Replace paper roll with a new one. Expected: Clear, legible ECG printout. Why it matters: Dirty print heads or degraded paper affect output quality.

steps.title: Power Cycle the Device
steps.instructions: Restart the EKG machine. Turn off the unit. Wait 10–15 seconds. Power back on and retry printing. Expected: Printer resets and functions normally. Why it matters: Clears minor system or printer communication faults.

CCR.Complaint: EKG machine not printing; paper not feeding during ECG acquisition.
CCR.Cause: Paper roll installed incorrectly, preventing proper feed.
CCR.Resolution: Reinstalled paper correctly and verified proper feed and print function.

helpfulDetails: Alarm behavior or error messages observed
Indicator lights or printer status messages

title: Schiller CARDIOVIT AT Series Electrocardiograph (EKG) Machine - Network or EMR Connectivity Failure
description: Troubleshooting guide for ECG devices not transmitting data to EMR due to network or configuration issues.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Do not delay ECG acquisition due to connectivity issues. If needed, print or store the ECG locally.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Issue
steps.instructions: Confirm ECG saves locally but does not export, check for error messages, and determine if failure is intermittent or complete.

steps.title: Check Physical Network Connection (Wired Systems)
steps.instructions: Ensure Ethernet cable is seated, check link lights, swap cable, and test different wall port.

steps.title: Verify Wi-Fi Connection (If Applicable)
steps.instructions: Confirm Wi-Fi enabled, check signal strength, reconnect to correct SSID.

steps.title: Confirm Network Configuration Settings
steps.instructions: Verify valid IP address, subnet, gateway, and DHCP/static configuration.

steps.title: Check EMR / Server Configuration Settings
steps.instructions: Verify server IP/hostname, port, protocol, department assignment, and device ID.

steps.title: Attempt Test Transmission
steps.instructions: Send a test ECG and observe for errors, success, or delays.

steps.title: Check for Network-Wide Issues
steps.instructions: Determine if other devices are affected and involve IT if necessary.

steps.title: Power Cycle the Device
steps.instructions: Restart the device to clear temporary communication faults.

CCR.Complaint: EKG not transmitting studies to EMR; staff reports export failures.
CCR.Cause: Misconfigured network settings with incorrect gateway address.
CCR.Resolution: Corrected network configuration and verified successful test transmission.

helpfulDetails: Final device status (working or removed from service)
